destinations.evidence: The registry stores only a capacity and evicts only the oldest inactive executor when capacity is reached; there is no TTL field or time-based cleanup in registration.

equivalence_argument: ExecutorRegistry entries are not expired by registration_time; registration_time is used only to choose among inactive entries after capacity has already been reached.
rationale: The primary diff removes executor_ttl and last_cleanup fields, removes cleanup_if_needed/cleanup_expired, and stops calling cleanup during register_executor. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: ExecutorRegistry entries must not be removed solely because their registration_time exceeds a TTL; registration should only evict when capacity is reached, using the existing oldest-inactive removal policy.
suggested_falsification_tests: Register an inactive executor with an old registration_time, simulate time passing well beyond the former TTL, then register another executor while below capacity and assert the old entry remains; separately assert capacity overflow can still remove an old inactive executor.
summary: ExecutorRegistry has no TTL expiry path.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Register an inactive executor, let registration_time become old, then register below capacity and assert it remains; fill capacity and assert only inactive capacity eviction can remove an entry.
title: Executor registry has no TTL expiry
